description: Create a chapter content slide for Chapter 5 with a left photo panel, two-column explanatory text, and a horizontal bar chart showing automation and advanced analytics adoption in risk management.

header.headline: Chapter 5
header.sub: Second Annual EY/EIU Global Insurance Risk Management Survey

main.body: Similar to how technology has transformed the business of insurance, it’s transforming the practice of risk management. The results show that risk management teams are advancing their use of automation and advanced analytics (including AI) in their own operations but still have a significant way to go. Less than a quarter (23%) have deployed the technology for at least one use case, while nearly twice as many (44%) are in exploration mode and a third (33%) have not implemented automation.

items.text: Similar to how technology has transformed the business of insurance, it’s transforming the practice of risk management.
Risk management teams are advancing their use of automation and advanced analytics (including AI) in their own operations but still have a significant way to go.
Less than a quarter (23%) have deployed the technology for at least one use case.
44% are in exploration mode and 33% have not implemented automation.

center.title: Figure 12: To what extent has your institution’s risk management function implemented risk management capabilities enabled by automation or advanced analytics (including AI/GenAI and ML)?

series.label: Implemented one or more automation or advanced analytics use cases to enable risk management activities or enable the risk function
Exploring automation or advanced analytics use cases, but not yet implemented
Have not implemented automation or advanced analytics for risk management
